message:
feat(history): Complete command history system with stats, export, and pack analysis

SUMMARY
=======
This commit transforms the telemetry module into a comprehensive command
history system, renaming "telemetry" to "history" throughout and adding
three major features: statistics computation, data export, and pack
effectiveness analysis.

BREAKING CHANGES
================
- Module renamed: `telemetry` → `history`
- Types renamed: `TelemetryDb` → `HistoryDb`, `TelemetryError` → `HistoryError`,
  `TelemetryConfig` → `HistoryConfig`, `TelemetryRedactionMode` → `HistoryRedactionMode`,
  `TelemetryWriter` → `HistoryWriter`, `TelemetryFlushHandle` → `HistoryFlushHandle`
- Environment variables renamed: `DCG_TELEMETRY_DB` → `DCG_HISTORY_DB`,
  `DCG_TELEMETRY_DISABLED` → `DCG_HISTORY_DISABLED`
- Database filename: `telemetry.db` → `history.db`

NEW FEATURES
============

1. Statistics Computation (`dcg history stats`)
   - HistoryStats: Aggregated statistics for a configurable time window
   - OutcomeStats: Breakdown by allow/deny/warn/bypass outcomes
   - PerformanceStats: P50/P95/P99/max evaluation latency percentiles
   - PatternStat: Top triggered patterns with counts and pack IDs
   - ProjectStat: Command distribution by working directory
   - AgentStat: Command distribution by agent type
   - StatsTrends: Period-over-period comparison (--trends flag)
   - Methods: compute_stats(), compute_stats_with_trends()

2. Export Functionality (`dcg history export`)
   - ExportOptions: Format selection and compression control
   - ExportFilters: Date range, outcome, and limit filtering
   - ExportedData: Metadata wrapper for JSON exports
   - Formats: JSON (with metadata), JSONL (streaming), CSV
   - Gzip compression support (--compress flag)
   - Methods: export_commands(), query_commands_for_export()
   - Proper CSV escaping for special characters

3. Pack Effectiveness Analysis (`dcg history analyze`)
   - PatternEffectiveness: Per-pattern bypass rate analysis
   - PotentialGap: Detection of dangerous allowed commands
   - RecommendationType: Categorized recommendations (relax/enable/disable/add/tuning)
   - PackRecommendation: Actionable suggestions with config snippets
   - PackEffectivenessAnalysis: Complete analysis result structure
   - Methods: analyze_pack_effectiveness(), query_pattern_effectiveness(),
     categorize_patterns(), query_active_packs(), find_coverage_gaps(),
     generate_recommendations()
   - Thresholds: >20% bypass rate flagged as aggressive, >10 triggers for high-value

CLI ADDITIONS
=============
New `dcg history` subcommand with four actions:

  dcg history stats [--days N] [--trends] [--json]
    Show history statistics with optional trend comparison

  dcg history prune --older-than-days N [--dry-run] [--yes]
    Remove old entries with safety confirmation

  dcg history export [-o PATH] [-f json|jsonl|csv] [--outcome X]
                     [--since DATE] [--until DATE] [--limit N] [--compress]
    Export command history to various formats

  dcg history analyze [--days N] [--json] [--recommendations-only]
                      [--false-positives] [--gaps]
    Analyze pack effectiveness and generate actionable recommendations

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
======================
- Statistics use SQLite aggregation for efficient computation
- Percentile calculation handles edge cases (empty data, single values)
- Export streaming uses minimal memory for large datasets
- Analysis queries use subqueries to avoid N+1 problems
- All new types implement Serialize for JSON output
- Backward compatibility: Config falls back gracefully if history section missing

FILES CHANGED
=============
- src/telemetry/schema.rs: +1800 lines (types, methods, tests)
- src/telemetry/mod.rs: Type renames and new exports
- src/config.rs: TelemetryConfig → HistoryConfig with all field renames
- src/lib.rs: Module alias via #[path] directive, updated re-exports
- src/main.rs: Updated to use history module and types
- src/cli.rs: New HistoryAction enum and handler functions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
